Artifact viewer · Engine B · Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol

The Ghana briefings, embedded.

Four allocation cartographies prepared for sovereign audiences. Each briefing is a published Power In Numbers deliverable. Use the tabs to move between them; use Open in new tab to read at full width.

Engagement: Ghana Briefings: 4 of 4
§ 01 The four briefings
Engine B · Sovereign Protocol

Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol — Domestic Resource Mobilisation

Allocation analysis of Ghana’s domestic resource mobilisation envelope. Four allocation options costed; counterfactuals costed alongside.

  • AudienceFiscal authority
  • Period2026 — 2028
  • Deliverable Open in new tab ↗
Engine B · Sovereign Protocol

Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol — Health Financing

Allocation cartography for the health-financing line item. The principal is a Ministry of Finance audience; the analysis is the sovereign analysing itself.

  • AudienceMinistry of Finance
  • PeriodCurrent cycle
  • Deliverable Open in new tab ↗
Engine B · Sovereign Protocol

Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol — Ghana Stabilisation Fund

Sovereign-fund analysis structured for an oversight body. Counterfactual deployments costed inline.

  • AudienceOversight body
  • PeriodCurrent cycle
  • Deliverable Open in new tab ↗
Engine B · Sovereign Protocol

Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol — Annual Budget Funding Amount

Annual Budget Funding Amount allocation analysis with explicit counterfactual deployments.

  • AudienceFiscal authority
  • PeriodCurrent cycle
  • Deliverable Open in new tab ↗
§ 02 Read further

Where this fits.

These four briefings are the Engine B half of the Ghana engagement. The Market Entry Protocol Stage-3 report \u2014 Engine A \u2014 is published in full on the Ghana case-study page, which also walks the throughline back to 2011.

  • Case study Ghana market entry The full case, with Engine A and Engine B threaded through one principal across fifteen years.
  • Methodology The three engines How the Market Entry Protocol, the Sovereign Protocol, and VERIDEX BI compose. Engine extensions G·24 — G·27.
  • Track record · 2010 Go Local Magazine The 2011 print profile that anchors the principal\u2019s relationship with the firm.
Power In Numbers A strategic consultancy. Practiced as decision cartography.
